# Build Guide — What is Hand-Maintained vs Generated

This document records every file in the project and whether it is
**hand-edited** (you change it directly) or **generated** (the build
pipeline overwrites it; never edit by hand).

---

## The single source of truth

| File | Hand-edited | Generated by |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| **`citation.csl.json`** | ✅ YES | — |

Everything else in the bibliography pipeline flows from this one file.

---

## Generated files (do NOT hand-edit)

These files are **regenerated on every `pnpm run build`**. If you edit
them, your changes will be overwritten on the next build.

| File | Regenerated by | Source of truth |
|------|----------------|-----------------|
| `src/bibliography.bib` | `scripts/tasks/citation.ts` → `@citation-js/plugin-bibtex` | `citation.csl.json` |
| `CITATION.cff` | `scripts/tasks/citation.ts` → `@citation-js/plugin-cff` | `citation.csl.json` + `package.json` metadata |
| `.zenodo.json` | `scripts/tasks/citation.ts` (native sync) | `CITATION.cff` |
| `build/document-v*.pdf` | `scripts/tasks/compile.ts` → docker `pdflatex` + `biber` | `src/chapters/*.tex` + `src/bibliography.bib` |
| `checksums.txt` | `scripts/tasks/checksums.ts` | `build/document-v*.pdf` |
| `images/fig*.pdf` | `scripts/figures/CW3_all_figures.py` | Python code |

---

## Hand-edited files (safe to modify)

These are the files you or the author maintain directly.

| File | Purpose | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| `citation.csl.json` | **THE** bibliography source of truth | 76 entries. Edit this to change any citation. Rebuild to propagate. |
| `main.tex` | Document preamble + `\input{}` chain | amsart class, author block, `\addbibresource` |
| `src/chapters/*.tex` | Paper content (8 chapters + abstract + ack/disclosure) | Edit prose, equations, references here |
| `src/chapters/references.tex` | Verbatim copy of the original hardcoded bib | Historical reference only; NOT compiled |
| `lean/CW3_Backing.lean` | Lean 4 formal verification | Edit for proof changes |
| `tests/CW3_backing_verify.py` | Python numerical backing | 41 labelled checks |
| `package.json` | Project metadata, scripts, versions | `metadata.abstract` and `metadata.zenodo_description` feed into `CITATION.cff` |
| `.zenodo.json` (only the `description` field) | Override the auto-generated Zenodo description | Only needed if you want a different description than what `CITATION.cff` produces. Normally not hand-edited. |
| `README.md` | Project overview, badge, citation | Contains DOI badge, citation block, BibTeX block |
| `docs/TODO.md` | Open work items | Your notes |

---

## How to change the bibliography

1. Edit `citation.csl.json` (the single source of truth).
2. Run `pnpm run build`.
3. Done — `src/bibliography.bib`, `CITATION.cff`, `.zenodo.json`, and
   the PDF all update automatically.

Do **not** edit `src/bibliography.bib` directly — it will be overwritten.

---

## How to change the paper content

Edit the appropriate file in `src/chapters/` and run `pnpm run build`.
The bibliography is not affected; only the PDF regenerates.

---

## How to change figures

Edit `scripts/figures/CW3_all_figures.py` and run
`pnpm run generate:figures` (or `pnpm run build:full`).
Figures are saved as PDF into `images/`.

---

## What `pnpm run build` does (step by step)

```
pnpm run build
  → cleanup:     removes build/document-v*.pdf and src/bibliography.bib
  → citation.ts: reads citation.csl.json
                  → generates src/bibliography.bib (via citation-js bibtex plugin)
                  → generates CITATION.cff (via citation-js cff plugin)
                  → generates .zenodo.json (native mapping)
  → compile.ts:  docker pdflatex pass 1 → biber → pdflatex pass 2 → pdflatex pass 3
                  → produces build/document-v<version>.pdf
  → checksums.ts: SHA-256 of the PDF
```

---

## What `pnpm run verify` does

```
pnpm run verify
  → verify:py:   python tests/CW3_backing_verify.py  (41 equation-backed checks)
  → verify:lean: cd lean && lake build CW3_Backing   (Lean 4 formal proof, 8249 jobs)
```

These do NOT touch the bibliography or the PDF — they only run
verification suites against the existing Lean and Python sources.
